objective: Recognize that rectangles with the same area can have different perimeters, and find which has the smallest perimeter

learn.title: 같은 넓이라도 둘레가 다를 수 있다
learn.content: Rectangles with the SAME area can have DIFFERENT perimeters. Example — all have area 12 sq cm: 12×1 → P=26. 6×2 → P=16. 4×3 → P=14. The shape closest to a square has the SMALLEST perimeter! 예: Three rectangles all have area 18 sq cm: 18×1, 9×2, 6×3. Which has the smallest perimeter? → 6×3 with perimeter 18 cm. (18×1→P=38, 9×2→P=22, 6×3→P=18. Closest to square = smallest P.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.6 'Same Area, Different Perimeters' pp.433-436
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Lesson 20 — Same Area, Different Perimeters
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 약수 쌍으로 직사각형 나열
learn.content: To find all rectangles with the same area, list factor pairs. For area=24: (24,1), (12,2), (8,3), (6,4). Perimeters: 50, 28, 22, 20. The (6,4) pair has the smallest perimeter — closest to a square. 예: Area = 36 sq cm. Factor pairs: (36,1), (18,2), (12,3), (9,4), (6,6). Which has the smallest perimeter? → 6×6 with perimeter 24 cm. (36×1→74, 18×2→40, 12×3→30, 9×4→26, 6×6→24. Square wins!)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.6 'Same Area, Different Perimeters' pp.433-436
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Lesson 20 — Same Area, Different Perimeters
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 펜스 최소 — 정사각형이 답
learn.content: The rectangle with the GREATEST perimeter (for same area) is the most elongated — long and thin. The one with SMALLEST perimeter is closest to a square. Real-world use: fencing a field — square shape uses least fence for same area. 예: A farmer needs 20 sq m of garden. Options: 20×1, 10×2, 5×4. Which uses the LEAST fencing? → 5×4 uses least fencing with perimeter 18 m. (20×1→42, 10×2→24, 5×4→18. Closest to square = least fencing.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.6 'Same Area, Different Perimeters' pp.433-436
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Lesson 20 — Same Area, Different Perimeters
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 정사각형이 최소 둘레 — 핵심 원리
learn.content: 주어진 넓이에서 가장 작은 둘레를 가지는 직사각형은 정사각형. 예: A=36 → 6×6 (P=24)가 최소. 36×1(P=74), 18×2(P=40), 12×3(P=30), 9×4(P=26)보다 작음. 🔍 직관: 균형 잡힌 모양일수록 가장자리 짧음.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.6 'Same Area, Different Perimeters' pp.433-436
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Lesson 20 — Same Area, Different Perimeters
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 가늘고 긴 모양 = 최대 둘레
learn.content: 같은 넓이에서 가장 큰 둘레는 1×넓이 (가장 가늘고 긴) 모양. 예: A=24 → 24×1(P=50)이 최대. 6×4(P=20)와 비교하면 2.5배. 🔍 같은 면적이라도 길게 늘이면 펜스 더 많이 필요.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.6 'Same Area, Different Perimeters' pp.433-436
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Lesson 20 — Same Area, Different Perimeters
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 약수 쌍 루틴 — 모든 직사각형 찾기
learn.content: 넓이 A → l × w = A 인 정수 쌍 모두 나열 (l ≥ w). 예: A=20 → 약수쌍: (20,1), (10,2), (5,4). 둘레 42, 24, 18.   단계: ① A의 약수 찾기. ② l≥w 쌍으로 묶기. ③ 각 P 계산. 🔍 정사각형 가능 (A가 제곱수일 때)? → 그게 최소 P.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.6 'Same Area, Different Perimeters' pp.433-436
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Lesson 20 — Same Area, Different Perimeters
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 실생활 결정 — 펜스 절약
learn.content: 넓이 36 m² 정원, 펜스를 최소로 쓰려면?   옵션: 36×1(P=74m), 12×3(P=30m), 9×4(P=26m), 6×6(P=24m). 정답: 6×6 정사각형, 펜스 24 m. 🔍 같은 넓이 보장 + 펜스 최소화 = 정사각형. (반대로 가늘게 만들면 펜스 낭비.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.6 'Same Area, Different Perimeters' pp.433-436
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Lesson 20 — Same Area, Different Perimeters
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 두 원리 요약 — L5 vs L6
learn.content: L5: 같은 둘레 → 정사각형 = 최대 넓이 / 길쭉 = 최소 넓이. L6: 같은 넓이 → 정사각형 = 최소 둘레 / 길쭉 = 최대 둘레. 🔍 두 원리 공통점: '정사각형이 가장 효율적'. 응용: 펜스 절약(같은 면적), 면적 최대(같은 펜스) — 둘 다 정사각형 답.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.6 'Same Area, Different Perimeters' pp.433-436
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Lesson 20 — Same Area, Different Perimeters
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

try.question: Which statement is always true for rectangles with the same area?
choices: The widest rectangle has the smallest perimeter
The rectangle closest to a square has the smallest perimeter
They all have the same perimeter
try.answer: The rectangle closest to a square has the smallest perimeter
try.hint: Think about which shape is most compact.
guided_steps: Step 1: A square has equal sides — most compact shape.
Step 2: More compact = smaller perimeter for same area.

practice_r3.question: Why does the square always have the smallest perimeter among rectangles with the same area? Explain with an example.
practice_r3.answer: The square is most compact — sides are as equal as possible, minimizing perimeter. Example Area=25: 25×1→P=52, 5×5→P=20. Equal sides = minimum perimeter.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.6 'Same Area, Different Perimeters' pp.433-436
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Lesson 20 — Same Area, Different Perimeters
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r3.question: A rectangle has area 48 sq cm and perimeter 28 cm. What are the dimensions? Show your work.
practice_r3.answer: l×w=48 and (l+w)×2=28 → l+w=14. Try pairs that multiply to 48 and add to 14: 6+8=14, 6×8=48 ✓. Length=8 cm, Width=6 cm.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.6 'Same Area, Different Perimeters' pp.433-436
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Lesson 20 — Same Area, Different Perimeters
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r3.question: Tom says: 'Same area means same perimeter.' Lin disagrees. Who is correct?
choices: A. Tom 맞음 — 둘 다 항상 같음
practice_r3.explanation: 같은 넓이라도 둘레는 다양함. A=16 → 16×1 P=34, 8×2 P=20, 4×4 P=16. P 다름.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.6 'Same Area, Different Perimeters' pp.433-436
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Lesson 20 — Same Area, Different Perimeters
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r3.question: A 28 sq ft garden needs fencing. List ALL whole-number rectangle dimensions and their perimeters. Which uses the LEAST fence?
practice_r3.explanation: 28의 약수쌍: 1×28, 2×14, 4×7 (3개). 둘레 58, 32, 22. 7×4가 정사각에 가장 가까움 → 22 ft 최소. (28은 제곱수 아님 → 정사각 불가.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.10 Lesson 10.6 'Same Area, Different Perimeters' pp.433-436
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 7 Lesson 20 — Same Area, Different Perimeters
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015
